name: Do I need a real lawyer if AI visibility services promise to handle my legal visibility case?
description: Yes—AI can't practice law or give advice; get a licensed attorney.[5]

mainEntity.name: Do I need a real lawyer if AI visibility services promise to handle my legal visibility case?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes—AI can't practice law or give advice; get a licensed attorney.[5]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, only licensed attorneys can provide legal advice; AI services cannot represent you.[5] Courts prohibit unlicensed practice, including AI-generated advice.[1][5] Key factors: AI hallucinations risk bad outcomes; privilege issues; ethical rules require verification.[2] Hire a bar-admitted lawyer; use AI visibility tools only for marketing, not litigation; verify all claims.[4] Real counsel ensures compliance.
